title: Anguina dicoelosperma
taxonomy:
Unfortunately, I could not find any information on the classification of Anguina dicoelosperma.

However, based on what I know about the genus Anguina, it belongs to the phylum Nematoda, class Secernentea, order Tylenchida, and family Anguinidae.

Anguina dicoelosperma specifically is a type of nematode, a small, unsegmented roundworm.

These nematodes are parasitic and often live in the tissues of plants, feeding on their nutrients.

Further specific classification details for Anguina dicoelosperma are not readily available.
benefits: Anguina dicoelosperma, commonly known as a type of nematode or parasitic worm, does not appear to have any well-documented health benefits. However, certain species of nematodes have been found to have potential health benefits, such as aiding in the breakdown of organic matter. They can also serve as a food source for other organisms. Some species of nematodes have been used in medical research to develop new treatments for certain diseases. More research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Anguina dicoelosperma.
